# Step 1 — Warm-Up Ramp (COMPLETE)

> **STATUS: COMPLETE** — Implemented during the M1.2.5 per-word adaptive pacing integration.
> All warm-up fields (`warmupProgress`, `isWarmingUp`), constants (`_warmupDurationMs = 10000`,
> `_warmupStartFraction = 0.80`), `play()` init/resume logic, and `_scheduleNext()` multiplier
> are already in `lib/core/word_timer.dart`. **Proceed to Step 2 (tests).**

> **Milestone**: M1.4 | **Epic**: E1.4.1 | **Feature**: F1.4.1.1
> **Depends on**: nothing (first step)
> **Parallel with**: Step 2 (tests — written against the contract independently), Step 4 (accessibility — zero file overlap)
> **Skills to load**: `flutter-managing-state`

Load shared context: [M1.4 reading polish context](../instructions/m1.4-reading-polish-context.instructions.md)

---

## Original Task (Already Done)

Add a warm-up ramp to `WordTimerNotifier` so the first ~10 seconds of reading play at reduced speed (80% of target WPM), linearly ramping to 100%. This gives ADHD/neurodivergent readers a gentle on-ramp into the reading flow instead of an abrupt start at full speed.

This is pure Dart — no Flutter imports, no widgets, no platform dependencies.

## Files to Modify

### 1. `lib/core/word_timer.dart`

#### Add warm-up fields to `WordTimerState`

Add these fields to the existing constructor and `copyWith`:

```dart
class WordTimerState {
  const WordTimerState({
    this.currentIndex = 0,
    this.totalWords = 0,
    this.isPlaying = false,
    this.wpm = 300,
    this.pacingConfig = defaultPacingConfig,
    this.warmupProgress = 0.0,   // NEW: 0.0 = start, 1.0 = complete
    this.isWarmingUp = false,    // NEW: true during the ramp period
  });

  // ... existing fields ...

  /// Warm-up progress from 0.0 (just started) to 1.0 (ramp complete).
  final double warmupProgress;

  /// Whether the warm-up ramp is currently active.
  final bool isWarmingUp;

  // Update copyWith to include warmupProgress and isWarmingUp
}
```

#### Add warm-up constants

Add these as static constants or top-level constants near the class:

```dart
/// Duration of the warm-up ramp in milliseconds.
const int _warmupDurationMs = 10000;

/// Starting speed fraction (80% of target WPM = 1.25× interval).
const double _warmupStartFraction = 0.80;
```

#### Add warm-up state to the notifier

Add a private field to track warmup start time:

```dart
DateTime? _warmupStart;
```

#### Modify `play()`

When `play()` is called:
- If this is a **first play** (not a resume from pause): reset `_warmupStart = DateTime.now()`, set `isWarmingUp = true`, `warmupProgress = 0.0`
- If this is a **resume from pause** AND warmup was not complete: resume from where warmup left off (do NOT reset `_warmupStart` — compute remaining time from `warmupProgress`)
- If warmup was already complete before pause: no warmup — `isWarmingUp = false`

Implementation approach:
```dart
void play() {
  if (state.isFinished || state.totalWords == 0) return;

  // P18 Grade C — auto-rewind on resume (existing, preserve)
  if (_wasPaused && _hasPlayedOnce) {
    final rewindTarget = (state.currentIndex - RunThruTiming.autoRewindWords)
        .clamp(0, state.totalWords - 1);
    state = state.copyWith(currentIndex: rewindTarget);
    _wasPaused = false;
  }

  // Warm-up ramp logic
  if (!_hasPlayedOnce) {
    // First play — start warmup from scratch
    _warmupStart = DateTime.now();
    state = state.copyWith(isWarmingUp: true, warmupProgress: 0.0);
  } else if (_wasPausedDuringWarmup()) {
    // Resume during warmup — adjust _warmupStart to account for elapsed progress
    final remainingMs = (_warmupDurationMs * (1.0 - state.warmupProgress)).round();
    _warmupStart = DateTime.now().subtract(Duration(milliseconds: _warmupDurationMs - remainingMs));
  }

  _hasPlayedOnce = true;
  state = state.copyWith(isPlaying: true);
  _startTimer();
}
```

Add a helper to check if warmup was in progress when paused:
```dart
bool _wasPausedDuringWarmup() => _wasPaused && state.warmupProgress < 1.0 && state.isWarmingUp;
```

#### Modify `_scheduleNext()`

After computing the per-word interval (from `durationForWord()`), apply the warm-up multiplier:

```dart
void _scheduleNext() {
  final base = state.intervalMs;
  final word = _wordAt?.call(state.currentIndex);
  final next = _wordAt?.call(state.currentIndex + 1);
  final interval = word == null
      ? base
      : durationForWord(word, nextWord: next, baseIntervalMs: base, config: state.pacingConfig);

  // ── Warm-up ramp ──
  var adjustedInterval = interval;
  if (_warmupStart != null) {
    final elapsedMs = DateTime.now().difference(_warmupStart!).inMilliseconds;
    final progress = (elapsedMs / _warmupDurationMs).clamp(0.0, 1.0);

    if (progress < 1.0) {
      // Linear ramp: speedFraction goes from _warmupStartFraction to 1.0
      // Interval multiplier is 1/speedFraction (slower speed = longer interval)
      final speedFraction = _warmupStartFraction + (1.0 - _warmupStartFraction) * progress;
      adjustedInterval = (interval / speedFraction).round();
      state = state.copyWith(warmupProgress: progress, isWarmingUp: true);
    } else if (state.isWarmingUp) {
      // Warmup just completed
      _warmupStart = null;
      state = state.copyWith(warmupProgress: 1.0, isWarmingUp: false);
    }
  }

  var delay = adjustedInterval;

  // Drift correction (existing — preserve exactly)
  if (_isFirstTick) {
    _isFirstTick = false;
  } else if (_lastTick != null) {
    final now = DateTime.now();
    final elapsed = now.difference(_lastTick!).inMilliseconds;
    final drift = elapsed - adjustedInterval;
    delay = (adjustedInterval - drift).clamp(1, adjustedInterval * 2);
  }

  _timer = Timer(Duration(milliseconds: delay), _tick);
}
```

#### Modify `loadDocument()`

Reset warmup state when a new document is loaded:

```dart
void loadDocument(int totalWords, {int startIndex = 0}) {
  _stopTimer();
  _wasPaused = false;
  _hasPlayedOnce = false;
  _wordAt = null;
  _warmupStart = null;  // NEW
  state = WordTimerState(
    totalWords: totalWords,
    currentIndex: startIndex,
    pacingConfig: state.pacingConfig,
    // warmupProgress and isWarmingUp default to 0.0 and false
  );
}
```

#### `seekTo()`, `restartCurrentSentence()`, `resumeFromContextReveal()`

These methods should NOT reset the warmup. The user is repositioning within the same reading session — the warm-up is about session start, not position.

## What NOT to Do

- Do NOT modify `lib/core/orp.dart` or any file in `lib/three_d/`
- Do NOT change gesture classifier thresholds
- Do NOT add Flutter imports — this file is pure Dart + Riverpod
- Do NOT add a warmup toggle to settings (that comes later if needed)
- Do NOT change the per-word pacing algorithm — warmup multiplies the FINAL interval
- Do NOT break existing auto-rewind behavior (P18 Grade C)
- Do NOT break existing drift correction logic

## Verification

```powershell
dart analyze lib/core/word_timer.dart
flutter test test/core/word_timer_test.dart
```

Both must pass with zero issues. Existing word_timer_test.dart tests must not regress.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `WordTimerState` has `warmupProgress` (double, 0.0–1.0) and `isWarmingUp` (bool) fields
- [ ] `copyWith()` includes both new fields
- [ ] First `play()` starts warm-up at 80% speed (1.25× interval)
- [ ] Speed linearly ramps to 100% over 10 seconds
- [ ] After 10 seconds, `isWarmingUp` is `false` and `warmupProgress` is `1.0`
- [ ] Pause during warm-up preserves `warmupProgress`; resume continues from that point
- [ ] `loadDocument()` resets warm-up state
- [ ] `seekTo()` / `restartCurrentSentence()` / `resumeFromContextReveal()` do NOT reset warm-up
- [ ] Existing auto-rewind (P18) still works
- [ ] Existing drift correction still works
- [ ] Existing per-word adaptive pacing (M1.2.5) still works — warmup multiplies the final interval
- [ ] `dart analyze lib/core/word_timer.dart` reports zero issues
- [ ] `flutter test test/core/word_timer_test.dart` — all existing tests pass
- [ ] All public symbols have dartdoc comments

## You May Refuse If

- The `_scheduleNext()` method signature has changed from what's shown in the shared context
- `WordTimerState` has been restructured (e.g., moved to a different file or using freezed)
- Any of the existing test assertions would become invalid due to warmup affecting timing

List the ambiguities instead of guessing.
